parsed_generation.narration: First, A person making a peanut butter and jelly sandwich stands at a kitchen counter with bread, a jar of peanut butter, a jar of jelly, and a knife, the camera at eye level capturing a medium view to establish the scene. Finally, the person brings the second slice over, completes the sandwich, and presents the finished PB&J on a plate in a steady, frontal view, signaling completion of the task.

feedback:
The output stays focused on a single person making PB&J and uses timestamped shot blocks, but it diverges from the gold standard by not showing the bread layout, the two spreading actions on separate slices, or a final diagonal cut. To align, structure two clearly defined shots: [00:00-00:04] bread slices laid out with peanut butter on one and jelly on the other; [00:04-00:08] stack the slices and cut the sandwich diagonally, presenting it on a plate. Use more action-first, concrete verbs to mirror the gold standard’s cinematic style.

strengths: Uses clean, timestamped shot blocks with camera-angle labels.
Maintains a single-subject focus throughout the sequence.
fixes: Add explicit bread layout and two-slice preparation: spread peanut butter on one slice and jelly on the other.
Show the assembly step and final presentation: bring slices together, cut diagonally, plate the sandwich.
Adopt concise, action-forward wording to mirror the gold standard (e.g., 'Bread slices laid out', 'Peanut butter spread', 'Jelly spread', 'Sandwich assembled', 'Diagonal cut').
